title_en: Introduction: Al-Baghawi and the Explanation of the Sunnah
content_en:
Sharh al-Sunnah (The Explanation of the Sunnah) by Abu Muhammad Husayn ibn Masud al-Baghawi (436–516 AH / 1044–1122 CE) is one of the most comprehensive hadith collections of the fifth Islamic century. Al-Baghawi was a major scholar of both hadith and Shafi'i jurisprudence in Khurasan, earning the titles Muhyi al-Sunnah (the reviver of the Sunnah) and Rukn al-Din (the pillar of religion) for his scholarship. Sharh al-Sunnah represents his attempt to compile the most important hadiths relevant to all areas of Islamic law and belief, accompanied by explanations of their legal and religious significance.

The work is not a systematic legal treatise like the Muhadhdhab or the Minhaj but a thematic hadith collection with commentary — the model goes back to the musannaf tradition of organizing hadiths by legal topic. Al-Baghawi organizes his collection by subject, presenting the relevant hadiths for each topic of worship, law, ethics, and belief along with explanations of difficult vocabulary, legal implications, and scholarly disagreements about interpretation and application.

Al-Baghawi's Position in Hadith Scholarship

Al-Baghawi lived in Merv (in modern Turkmenistan), a major center of Shafi'i and Hanafi scholarship in the Islamic East. He was a student of the great Khurasani hadith and fiqh scholars of the late fifth century, and his works bridge the worlds of hadith transmission and legal scholarship in a way characteristic of the best scholars of his era. His tafsir, Maalim al-Tanzil, was one of the most widely used Quran commentaries in the Sunni tradition, reflecting the same combination of hadith knowledge and legal reasoning that characterizes Sharh al-Sunnah.

In hadith scholarship, al-Baghawi's approach was conservative and sound: he relied primarily on the established collections (al-Bukhari, Muslim, Abu Dawud, al-Tirmidhi, al-Nasai, and Ibn Majah) rather than more obscure sources, and he was careful to note the chains and grades of the hadiths he cited. This conservatism made Sharh al-Sunnah a reliable reference work — not a repository of unusual or questionable hadiths but a systematic presentation of the established hadith tradition organized for practical use.

Structure and Organization

Sharh al-Sunnah is organized into books covering the major areas of Islamic practice: the Book of Faith (Kitab al-Iman), covering theological beliefs; the Book of Knowledge (Kitab al-Ilm), covering the importance of learning and scholars; the Book of Purification; the Book of Prayer; the Book of Funerals; the Book of Zakat; the Book of Fasting; the Book of Hajj; and so on through the full range of Islamic law and ethics. The work runs to fifteen volumes in the standard modern edition, reflecting the comprehensive scope of al-Baghawi's undertaking.
content_id:
Syarh al-Sunnah (Penjelasan Sunnah) karya Abu Muhammad Husayn ibn Mas'ud al-Baghawi (436-516 H / 1044-1122 M) adalah salah satu koleksi hadis yang paling komprehensif dari abad kelima Islam. Al-Baghawi adalah seorang ulama besar baik dalam hadis maupun fikih Syafi'i di Khurasan, mendapat gelar Muhyi al-Sunnah (penghidup Sunnah) dan Rukn al-Din (tiang agama) atas keilmuannya. Syarh al-Sunnah merupakan upayanya untuk mengumpulkan hadis-hadis terpenting yang relevan untuk semua bidang hukum dan akidah Islam, disertai penjelasan tentang signifikansi hukum dan agamanya.

Karya ini bukan risalah hukum yang sistematis seperti al-Muhadhdhab atau al-Minhaj tetapi kumpulan hadis tematik dengan komentar — modelnya kembali ke tradisi musannaf dalam mengorganisasi hadis berdasarkan topik hukum. Al-Baghawi mengorganisasi koleksinya berdasarkan subjek, menyajikan hadis-hadis yang relevan untuk setiap topik ibadah, hukum, etika, dan akidah beserta penjelasan kosakata yang sulit, implikasi hukum, dan ketidaksepakatan keilmuan tentang tafsir dan penerapan.

Posisi Al-Baghawi dalam Keilmuan Hadis

Al-Baghawi tinggal di Merv (di Turkmenistan modern), pusat utama keilmuan Syafi'i dan Hanafi di belahan timur Islam. Ia adalah murid para ulama hadis dan fikih Khurasani terkemuka dari akhir abad kelima, dan karya-karyanya menjembatani dunia transmisi hadis dan keilmuan hukum. Dalam keilmuan hadis, pendekatan al-Baghawi bersifat konservatif dan kukuh: ia terutama mengandalkan koleksi-koleksi yang telah mapan (al-Bukhari, Muslim, Abu Dawud, al-Tirmidzi, al-Nasa'i, dan Ibn Majah) daripada sumber-sumber yang lebih tidak dikenal, dan ia berhati-hati untuk mencatat sanad dan derajat hadis-hadis yang ia kutip.

Struktur dan Organisasi

Syarh al-Sunnah diorganisasi ke dalam kitab-kitab yang mencakup bidang-bidang utama praktik Islam: Kitab al-Iman (mencakup akidah teologis); Kitab al-Ilm (mencakup pentingnya belajar dan ulama); Kitab al-Thaharah; Kitab al-Shalat; Kitab al-Jana'iz; Kitab al-Zakat; Kitab al-Shaum; Kitab al-Haji; dan seterusnya melalui seluruh rentang hukum dan etika Islam. Karya ini terdiri dari lima belas volume dalam edisi modern standar, mencerminkan ruang lingkup komprehensif dari usaha al-Baghawi.
content_ar: Sharh al-السنة (The Explanation of the السنة) by Abu محمد ﷺ Husayn ibn Masud al-Baghawi (436–516 AH / 1044–1122 CE) is one of the most comprehensive الحديث collections of the خامساً Islamic century. Al-Baghawi was a major scholar of both الحديث and الشافعي jurisprudence in Khurasan, earning the titles Muhyi al-السنة (the reviver of the السنة) and Rukn al-Din (the pillar of religion) for his scholarship. Sharh al-السنة represents his attempt to compile the most المهم hadiths relevant to all areas of Islamic law and الاعتقاد, accompanied by explanations of their legal and religious significance. The work is not a systematic legal treatise like the Muhadhdhab or the Minhaj but a thematic الحديث collection with commentary — the model goes back to the musannaf التراث of organizing hadiths by legal topic. Al-Baghawi organizes his collection by subject, presenting the relevant hadiths for each topic of worship, law, ethics, and الاعتقاد along with explanations of difficult vocabulary, legal implications, and العلمي disagreements about interpretation and application. Al-Baghawi's Position in الحديث Scholarship Al-Baghawi lived in Merv (in modern Turkmenistan), a major center of الشافعي and الحنفي scholarship in the Islamic East. He was a student of the great Khurasani الحديث and الفقه العلماء of the late خامساً century, and his works bridge the worlds of الحديث transmission and legal scholarship in a الطريق characteristic of the best العلماء of his era. His التفسير, Maalim al-Tanzil, was one of the most widely used القرآن الكريم commentaries in the Sunni التراث, reflecting the same combination of الحديث العلم and legal reasoning that characterizes Sharh al-السنة. In الحديث scholarship, al-Baghawi's approach was conservative and sound: he relied primarily on the established collections (البخاري, مسلم, أبو داود, al-الترمذي, al-النسائي, and ابن ماجه) rather than more obscure sources, and he was careful to note the chains and grades of the hadiths he cited. This conservatism made Sharh al-السنة a reliable reference work — not a repository of unusual or questionable hadiths but a systematic presentation of the established الحديث التراث organized for practical use. Structure and Organization Sharh al-السنة is organized into الكتب covering the major areas of Islamic practice: the الكتاب of الإيمان ( Kitab al-الإيمان ), covering theological beliefs; the الكتاب of العلم ( Kitab al-Ilm ), covering the importance of learning and العلماء; the الكتاب of Purification; the الكتاب of الصلاة; the الكتاب of Funerals; the الكتاب of الزكاة; the الكتاب of الصيام; the الكتاب of الحج; and لذا on through the full range of Islamic law and ethics. The work runs to fifteen volumes in the standard modern edition, reflecting the comprehensive scope of al-Baghawi's undertaking.
